questions.question: Who headed the Diwan-i-Arz and was responsible for managing soldiers, branding horses, and administering war provisions?
questions.solution: The Diwan-i-Arz was the military department of the Delhi Sultanate. It was headed by the Ariz-i-Mumalik. His primary duties included the recruitment of troops, maintaining the descriptive rolls of soldiers (Huliyah), branding of horses (Dagh), and ensuring the distribution of war provisions and equipment. He acted as the controller general of the military but was not necessarily the commander-in-chief in the field.

questions.question: Who piloted Vasco da Gama’s ship to Calicut during his first successful voyage to India in 1498?
questions.solution: When Vasco da Gama reached the coast of East Africa (Malindi) during his 1498 voyage, he hired a skilled Gujarati pilot named Abdul Majid (also identified as Ahmad ibn Majid in some traditions). Abdul Majid used his expert knowledge of the monsoon winds and the Indian Ocean to successfully guide the Portuguese fleet to Calicut.

questions.question: Which city became the capital of Vatsaraja during the expansion of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty?
questions.solution: Vatsaraja was a powerful ruler of the Gurjara-Pratihara dynasty who reigned in the late 8th century. During his expansionist phase, he consolidated power in the Malwa region and made Ujjain his capital. He is also noted for being one of the early contenders in the Tripartite Struggle for Kannauj.

questions.question: Babar, the first Mughal ruler, was related to two of the biggest conquerors of the medieval world. If he was related to the Mongol conqueror Genghis Khan on his maternal side, who was he related to from his paternal side?
questions.solution: Zahir-ud-din Muhammad Babur belonged to a high-born lineage. On his mother's side, he was a descendant of Genghis Khan, the Great Mongol leader. On his father's side, he was a fifth-generation descendant of the Turko-Mongol conqueror Timur (Tamerlane). Because of this heritage, the Mughals were technically Timurids.

questions.question: Rajendra Chola I is known for his naval expeditions on the revived Sri Vijaya empire of:
questions.solution: Rajendra Chola I launched a massive naval expedition in 1025 CE against the Srivijaya Empire, which was based in present-day Indonesia (Sumatra and Java). This was done to secure Chola trade routes to China and to punish the Srivijayan King for interfering with Indian merchant ships in the Straits of Malacca.

questions.question: On which of the following states of India did Siraj-ud- Daula, Mir Jafar and Mir Qasim rule in the 18ᵗʰ century?
questions.solution: Siraj-ud-Daula, Mir Jafar, and Mir Qasim were the Nawabs of Bengal during the 18th century. Their reigns were marked by intense conflict with the British East India Company, culminating in the Battle of Plassey (1757) and the Battle of Buxar (1764), which eventually led to British political control over India.

questions.question: Who among the following Delhi Sultans controlled the prices of food; horses, ponies, cattle and slaves; cloth and fruit; and articles for domestic consumption and personal use?
questions.solution: Alauddin Khalji introduced the most comprehensive Market Control Policy in medieval Indian history. To maintain a large standing army, he fixed the prices of almost all essential commodities, including food grains, livestock (horses, cattle, and slaves), and various consumer goods like cloth and fruit. He enforced these prices through strict supervision and severe punishments for hoarding or cheating.
